meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no testing knowledge (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no testing knowledge. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Master Testing for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop shipping broken code. Master SaaS testing with this advanced checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code. Perfect for freelancers scaling their output.
content.answer_block: Eliminate testing anxiety by integrating a pre-configured SaaS starter kit that automates your test suite generation. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural blueprints and Claude Code for autonomous test execution, you transform testing from a manual bottleneck into a competitive advantage, ensuring your client deliverables are bug-free and production-ready.
content.intro:
The midnight oil is burning, your client’s deadline is looming, and a critical bug just surfaced in production. You know your code works, but without a robust testing suite, you're flying blind. The fear of breaking features during a minor refactor is the silent killer of every freelancer's scalability.

But the era of manual QA is dead. By adopting a hardened SaaS starter kit, you shift from 'hoping' your code works to 'knowing' it does. This isn't just about writing tests; it's about building a resilient, automated pipeline that acts as your personal engineering team, allowing you to ship faster while maintaining the high-quality reputation your clients demand.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Initialize a modular SaaS foundation that includes pre-configured testing layers. This ensures your project structure is inherently testable from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready SaaS starter kit blueprint using Next.js, Supabase, and Tailwind. Include an integrated Vitest configuration with a modular directory structure for unit and integration tests.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize project structure based on the Antigravity blueprint provided.'

steps.title: Automate Unit Test Scaffolding
steps.description: Don't write tests from scratch. Use AI to scan your business logic and generate high-coverage test suites automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the src/services directory. For every function, generate a corresponding Vitest spec file covering positive, negative, and edge-case scenarios.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate missing unit tests for all files in src/services using Vitest.'

steps.title: Implement E2E User Flows
steps.description: Simulate real user interactions to ensure critical paths like authentication and billing remain functional across updates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create Playwright configuration for a standard SaaS onboarding flow: Sign-up, Stripe checkout, and Dashboard access. Include visual regression snapshots.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create Playwright e2e tests for the critical user onboarding path.'

steps.title: Integrate CI/CD Test Gates
steps.description: Configure your pipeline to reject any pull request that doesn't meet a minimum 80% code coverage threshold.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs Vitest and Playwright on push. Fail the build if coverage drops below 80%.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Configure .github/workflows/test.yml to enforce 80% coverage gate.'

steps.title: Autonomous Regression Analysis
steps.description: Use AI to analyze test failures and suggest immediate code fixes, drastically reducing debugging time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the failed test logs in the terminal. Identify the root cause in the source code and propose a fix.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Analyze recent test failures and apply the necessary hotfix to the codebase.'

pro_tips: Always mock external API calls in your unit tests to prevent hitting rate limits and incurring costs during development.
Use 'Test-Driven Development' (TDD) for complex business logic to force yourself to define requirements before writing code.
Keep your test data isolated using ephemeral database schemas for every test run to ensure a clean state.
Prioritize 'Happy Path' tests first to ensure the core value proposition of your SaaS works before chasing edge cases.
Set up a 'smoke test' suite that runs every 5 minutes in production to detect environment-specific issues before users do.
content.next_steps: After completing this checklist, integrate your repository with a monitoring tool like Sentry to catch runtime errors that tests might miss, completing your full-stack reliability loop.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to ship faster and sleep better. Stop spending hours debugging and start spending hours building features that scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
